meta.name: FFMQ-15 Five Facet Mindfulness Questionnaire
meta.description: The FFMQ-15 is a 15-item self-report measure of mindfulness assessing five facets: observing, describing, acting with awareness, non-judging, and non-reacting.
meta.author: Baer et al. (2006); 15-item: Gu et al. (2016)
meta.reference: Gu J, Strauss C, Crane C, et al. Examining the factor structure of the 39-item and 15-item versions of the Five Facet Mindfulness Questionnaire. Mindfulness. 2016;7(6):1441-1451.
meta.instruction: Please rate each of the following statements. Answer according to what really reflects your experience rather than what you think your experience should be.

questions.text: When I am walking, I deliberately notice the sensations of my body moving
I am good at finding words to describe my feelings
I do jobs or tasks automatically without being aware of what I am doing
I tell myself I should not be feeling the way I am feeling
When I have distressing thoughts or images, I am able to just notice them without reacting
I notice changes in smells and aromas
I can easily put my beliefs, opinions, and expectations into words
I do not pay attention to what I am doing because I am daydreaming, worrying, or otherwise distracted
I make judgments about whether my thoughts are good or bad
I watch my feelings without getting lost in them
I notice visual elements in art or nature, such as colors, shapes, textures, or patterns of light and shadow
Even when I am feeling terribly upset, I can find a way to put it into words
I find it difficult to stay focused on what is happening in the present
I believe some of my thoughts are abnormal or bad and I should not think that way
In difficult situations, I can pause without immediately reacting

ranges.label: Low Mindfulness
ranges.description: Your mindfulness level is low. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Try 5-10 minutes of mindfulness meditation daily
Consider a Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction program
Practice deliberate attention to everyday activities
Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support

ranges.label: Moderate Mindfulness
ranges.description: Your mindfulness is moderate with room for growth. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Review subscale scores for improvement areas
Continue regular mindfulness practice
Practice observing thoughts without judgment
Consider consulting a mental health professional
Identify your specific sources of concern

ranges.label: High Mindfulness
ranges.description: Your mindfulness level is high. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue your mindfulness practice
Explore advanced mindfulness techniques
Integrate mindfulness into more daily activities
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences

observing.label: Low
observing.description: Low awareness of sensory experiences. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support
Follow the treatment plan recommended by your professional
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
If you feel overwhelmed, call a mental health helpline for immediate support

observing.label: Moderate
observing.description: Sometimes notices sensory experiences. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Identify your specific sources of concern
Maintain a structured daily routine
Try relaxation and coping methods that suit you
Engage in meaningful activities and social interactions

observing.label: High
observing.description: Skilled at noticing sensory experiences. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

suggestions: Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support
Follow the treatment plan recommended by your professional
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
If you feel overwhelmed, call a mental health helpline for immediate support

suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Identify your specific sources of concern
Maintain a structured daily routine
Try relaxation and coping methods that suit you
Engage in meaningful activities and social interactions

describing.label: High

suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

suggestions: Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support
Follow the treatment plan recommended by your professional
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
If you feel overwhelmed, call a mental health helpline for immediate support

Identify your specific sources of concern
Maintain a structured daily routine
Try relaxation and coping methods that suit you
Engage in meaningful activities and social interactions

Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

suggestions: Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support
Follow the treatment plan recommended by your professional
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
If you feel overwhelmed, call a mental health helpline for immediate support

Identify your specific sources of concern
Maintain a structured daily routine

Engage in meaningful activities and social interactions

suggestions: Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support
Follow the treatment plan recommended by your professional
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
If you feel overwhelmed, call a mental health helpline for immediate support

Identify your specific sources of concern
